title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA expects nearly 1 million attendees for St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston on March 15.
Red Line to operate on a 'rush hour' schedule from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m., with potential bypass of Broadway Station.
Commuter Rail adding train cars; routes 9 and 11 temporarily suspended, with detours for routes 10, 16, 17, and 47.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate reached 81.3% in 2025, a historic high but 8 points below Massachusetts' 89.3% average.
Increased graduation rates for all BPS demographics included a 5% rise for English learners and students with disabilities.
BPS's dropout rate decreased to 3.6%, down 0.9 points, the lowest on record excluding the pandemic year of 2021.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins hold an 11-14-5 road record, hindering playoff aspirations despite a strong home performance this season.
The Bruins currently occupy the second wild-card spot in the Eastern Conference with a 35-22-6 record, closely trailed by the Blue Jackets.
Boston faces 12 remaining away games, including crucial matches against current playoff teams and two against Columbus.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart of Marblehead is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients at Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Wishart, 42, aims to raise funds to enhance adaptive sports programs and clinical care for children.
As Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine, Wishart focuses on integrating research into practice to benefit all patients.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children have died from the flu in Massachusetts this season, according to the state's Department of Public Health.
As of February 28, 298 adults and eight children have succumbed to influenza in Massachusetts.
Massachusetts flu vaccination rate is 38.1%, with experts advising vaccination for those six months and older.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks' Magic City promotion scheduled for March 16 after backlash and league concerns.
Luke Kornet's open letter criticized the Hawks' promotion, prompting discussions about family-friendliness and appropriate NBA partnerships.
The canceled event included Magic City themed chicken wings, merchandise, and a performance by hip-hop artist T.I.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal was unaware of Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman until recent highlights of a game against the Cavaliers.
Baylor Scheierman, a first-round 2024 pick, has played 59 games for Boston with an average of 4.8 points per game.
Scheierman's playing time hasn't decreased despite Jayson Tatum's return from injury and is noted for his competitive spirit by coach Joe Mazzulla.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urged Signature Aviation, operating at Hanscom Field, to cease supporting ICE deportation flights.
Signature Aviation is critical for ICE operations at Hanscom, having coordinated 132 deportation flights recently.
Healey condemned ICE, highlighting due process violations and called the deportations indiscriminate, affecting non-criminal immigrants.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell about 20 feet after a grate collapse outside Boston's Omni Parker House on Friday morning.
Boston Inspectional Services cited the hotel for a building code violation due to rotted supporting brackets causing the incident.
The hotel must hire a structural engineer to inspect vent grates and submit a report on necessary repairs.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to play music for customers at the gas pumps.
Kertzman has posted signs for over a year, finding one retired violist, with plans to start as weather warms.
The station already offers complimentary water and inspirational quotes, enhancing the customer experience.
